applied_optimizations.name: Triton elementwise fusion for div+GELU
applied_optimizations.description: Fused division by scalar and GELU activation into a single Triton kernel. Uses PyTorch's optimized F.linear for matmul. GELU implemented manually using tl.math.exp since tl.math.tanh is unavailable on ROCm. BLOCK_SIZE_N set to next power of 2 for AMD wavefront alignment (64-wide).

attempted_but_failed.name: Fused matmul+div+GELU Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton compilation errors with pointer shape mismatch - complex 2D tiled matmul had issues with pointer arithmetic and broadcasting on ROCm Triton
attempted_but_failed.error: Expected pointer argument to have shape ['constexpr[1]', 'constexpr[64]', 'constexpr[64]'] but got ['constexpr[64]', 'constexpr[64]']

top_bottlenecks: Elementwise operations (div + GELU) fused into single kernel

notes: PyTorch's F.linear is already highly optimized for large GEMM (8192x8192)
Main optimization opportunity is fusing elementwise ops to reduce memory bandwidth
ROCm Triton requires manual tanh implementation using tl.math.exp

attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all crashed/timed out with no output. The agent needs an extremely simple, paste-ready solution. Best so far is 60.10 from trial 1. Will provide complete file content to minimize ag
